Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

This article may be too long to read and navigate comfortably. Consider splitting content into sub-articles, condensing it, or adding subheadings. Please discuss this issue on the article's talk page. (August 2025)

This is a list of games for the PlayStation 4. The PlayStation 4 supports both physical and digital games. Physical games are sold on Blu-ray Disc and digital games can be purchased through the PlayStation Store.[a] See Arcade Archives and Arcade Game Series for a list of emulated arcade games that have been released for the PlayStation 4, and List of PlayStation 2 games for PlayStation 4 for PlayStation 2 games running on PlayStation 4 with an emulator. See List of PlayStation VR games for a larger range of dedicated PlayStation VR games.

List

[edit]

There are currently 3742 games on this list.[b]

Notes

[edit]
  1. ^ Games dated November 15, 2013 (North America), November 29, 2013 (Europe) and February 22, 2014 (Japan) are launch titles for the specified regions.
  2. ^ This number is always up to date by this script.

  145. ^ "Undernauts: Labyrinth of Yomi launches October 28 in the west, PS5 version in 2022". Gematsu. August 6, 2021. Archived from the original on August 8, 2021. Retrieved August 8, 2021.
  146. ^ Romano, Sal (31 January 2019). "Unexplored: Unlocked Edition launches February 19 for PS4, February 22 for Xbox One". Gematsu. Retrieved 26 March 2023.
  147. ^ Romano, Sal (8 September 2022). "Unexplored 2: The Wayfarer's Legacy coming to PS4 on September 14". Gematsu. Retrieved 26 March 2023.
  148. ^ "Vaporum: Lockdown launches September 15 for PC, Q4 2020 for Switch, and 2021 for PS4 and Xbox One". Gematsu. 31 August 2020. Archived from the original on 2 September 2020. Retrieved September 2, 2020.
  149. ^ "Squad reinforcement game Vaster Claws III: Dragon Slayer of the God World ~Offline Ver. coming to PS4 on September 25 in Japan". Gematsu. September 17, 2020. Archived from the original on September 18, 2020. Retrieved September 19, 2020.
  150. ^ "Voice of Cards: The Forsaken Maiden announced for PS4, Switch, and PC". Gematsu. February 4, 2022. Archived from the original on February 4, 2022. Retrieved February 4, 2022.
  151. ^ "void tRrLM(); // Void Terrarium launches July 10 in Europe, July 14 in North America". Gematsu. 31 March 2020. Archived from the original on 31 March 2020. Retrieved March 31, 2020.
  152. ^ "War Mongrels Is a Bleak World War 2 Game Coming to PS5 and PS4". Push Square. 13 August 2020. Archived from the original on 24 October 2020. Retrieved August 30, 2020.
  153. ^ Yin-Poole, Wesley (15 November 2021). "Warhammer 40,000: Battlesector launches on Xbox Game Pass and PlayStation in December". Eurogamer. Gamer Network. Archived from the original on 11 March 2023. Retrieved 11 March 2023.
  154. ^ "Dramatic comedy adventure game What Lies in the Multiverse launches in early 2022 for PS4, Xbox One, Switch, and PC". Gematsu. October 2021. Archived from the original on October 2, 2021. Retrieved October 3, 2021.
  155. ^ "Wing of Darkness launches February 25, 2021 for PS4, Switch, and PC". Gematsu. October 2, 2020. Archived from the original on October 2, 2020. Retrieved October 2, 2020.
  156. ^ L, Stefan (August 4, 2021). "World War Z Aftermath release date set for September". TheSixthAxis. Archived from the original on September 12, 2021. Retrieved September 12, 2021.
  157. ^ Ryan Clements. The Drop: New PlayStation Games for 8/29/2017 Archived 2018-12-15 at the Wayback Machine, playstation.com, August 25, 2017.
  158. ^ "Yo-kai Watch Jam: Yo-kai Academy Y – Waiwai Gakuen Seikatsu launches August 13 in Japan for Switch, in 2020 for PS4". Gematsu. 3 July 2020. Archived from the original on 4 July 2020. Retrieved July 4, 2020.
  159. ^ "Yomawari 3 announced for PS4, Switch". Gematsu. 2021-12-15. Archived from the original on 2021-12-16. Retrieved 2021-12-16.
  160. ^ Romano, Sal (15 December 2022). "Ys X: Nordics announced for PS5, PS4, and Switch". Gematsu. Archived from the original on 19 December 2022. Retrieved 15 December 2022.
  161. ^ a b "Yumeutsutsu Re:Master and Re:After launch April 23 in the west for Switch and PC, later for PS4 and PS Vita". Gematsu. 10 April 2020. Archived from the original on 10 April 2020. Retrieved April 10, 2020.
  162. ^ "Yurukill: The Calumniation Games for PS5, PS4, and Switch launches June 7, 2022 in North America, June 10 in Europe". Gematsu. December 9, 2021. Archived from the original on December 9, 2021. Retrieved December 9, 2021.
  163. ^ "The Drop: New PlayStation Games for 9/20/2016". PlayStation America. 18 September 2016. Archived from the original on 15 December 2018. Retrieved September 18, 2016.
  164. ^ "The Drop: New PlayStation Releases for December 24th, 2013". PlayStation Blog. Archived from the original on 11 November 2020. Retrieved 11 February 2026.

142. PlayStation Blog Road 96 release date PlayStation Blog. 12 April 2022. Retrieved May 8, 2022.
